audit_items.proof_trace: main.h is a header file containing only struct definitions, macro definitions, inline accessor functions (e.g. mbpf_class, is_mbpf_load), and function declarations — none of which perform dynamic memory allocation. Exhaustive grep for kmalloc/kzalloc/vmalloc/kcalloc/kstrdup across all .h files in the nfp/bpf directory returns zero matches. The universal quantifier 'forall alloc(p) in main.h' holds vacuously because the set of dynamic allocations is empty.
